title: Project Telos Fifteenth-Wave Cyclic Finite-Sum Replay Claims

claims.text: The fifteenth-wave package adds a bounded Lean cyclic finite-sum replay rung while keeping continuous periodic integration by parts NOT_REPLAYED and the parent Millennium problem UNVERIFIABLE.
claims.falsification: The package is missing, lacks the cyclic finite-sum replay artifact, or claims the continuous PDE theorem or Navier-Stokes parent problem is proved.

claims.text: The fifteenth-wave theorem is stored in a separate Lean file and preserves the fourteenth-wave PeriodicCancellationPreflight.lean hash at 77e5cba345adc1fd99003bfc17092750fddca1a9f879b4ac59f99e74c374a936.
claims.falsification: The fifteenth theorem mutates the fourteenth-wave source file, or the recorded fourteenth-wave source hash no longer matches.

claims.text: CyclicFiniteSumPreflight.lean compiles with Lean exit code 0 and replays lastValue, pathDiffSum, pathDiffSum_telescopes, and cyclic_pathDiffSum_cancels.
claims.falsification: The Lean file is missing, the replay receipt is missing, the command does not exit 0, or the package claims the discrete theorem proves the continuous PDE theorem.

claims.text: The fifteenth-wave source refresh retains 15 arXiv metadata rows, 15 unique IDs, verifies both Gather stores as MATCH, and demotes the rows to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY.
claims.falsification: The source demotion gate is missing, counts differ, store verification does not match, or metadata is promoted as paper truth.

claims.text: The website copy, official-copy scaffold, handoff, revision queue, and tooling assessment distinguish CYCLIC_SUM_REPLAY_MATCH from NOT_REPLAYED continuous theorem claims.
claims.falsification: Any updated publication surface collapses the finite cyclic-sum replay into a smooth periodic integration-by-parts proof or omits the NOT_REPLAYED boundary.

claims.text: The fifteenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries for Navier-Stokes, smooth periodic integration by parts, source truth, exhaustive coverage, native BuildLang relation receipts, and accepted-publication claims.
claims.falsification: The content queue is missing or omits those boundaries.
